title: cytisus ratisbonensis subsp. ruthenicus
intro: Cytisus ratisbonensis subsp. ruthenicus, also known as Russian broom or Ruthenic broom, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the pea family that is native to Eastern Europe and Western Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Cytisus ratisbonensis subsp. ruthenicus include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and act as a diuretic, making it a popular treatment for various ailments. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, skin conditions, and other health problems.
